They are a low-level backdoor — primitives for cleaning up bad things at the member/tag grain — and that's where they belong; the `l` family is for the first-class Lode lifecycle (capture, the read-only `divine` enumeration, and `banish`). The `divine`/`audit` split is **grain, not redundancy**: `divine` enumerates **Lodes** by touchmark (lifecycle-grain, `rbw-l*`); `audit`/`rekon` enumerate **members/tags within** a Lode (maintenance-grain, `rbw-i*`). Their fetched-side domain variants (Reliquary + Enshrinement) consolidate under Lode/touchmark addressing; the made-side Hallmark variants are out of scope, untouched.
- **Tabtarget shape is decided, not open:** per-kind capture verbs under the new `l` (Lode) command family — `rbw-lE/lF/lC/lU/lI` capture, `rbw-ld` divine (read-only show), `rbw-lB` banish — superseding the earlier "unified `rbw-dE` with a kind argument" framing. (These move out of the Depot `d` family where `enshrine`/`inscribe`/`yoke` live today.)
- **Election (`yoke` + the deferred substrate verb) lives with its consumer, NOT in `l` — lowercase, and does not commit.** Election mutates the *consumer* (vessel for build-time kinds, nameplate for substrate kinds), not the Lode — the touchmark is its *argument*, the consumer its *target* — so it belongs in the consumer's family, paralleling made-side hallmark election (which lives with the nameplate/kludge machinery, never with ark/hallmark). `l` stays reserved for GAR create/destroy of the Lode itself. Three axes separate election from `l`-capture: it targets the **consumer** (not the Lode); it is **lowercase** (cheap reversible config, not persistent GAR/cost state); and it is **operator-committed** — it writes the config and emits a "commit with your usual workflow" hint, never self-committing. That last is the near-universal RBK convention: `yoke` today is explicit — RBSDY, *"this primitive does not commit; the caller commits"* — and the only RBK bash command that self-commits is Marshal-Zero (`rblm`), a wholesale reset ceremony that still gates a clean tree first. Election also does **not self-gate** on a dirty tree; the clean-tree guard lives at the downstream consumer (the build reading the vessel, charge reading the nameplate — `rbob_bottle` already gates the nameplate clean). Pace-time: `yoke` moves out of Depot `d` and de-capitalizes (`rbw-dY` → a lowercase consumer-family colophon); exact destination letter is a layout choice. (Capture verbs raise no git question at all — they write GAR, not git-tracked config.)

## Discovery recipes — work to enumerate when paces approach

- **Verb redesign blast radius.** Scan `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSA*.adoc` and `RBSI*.adoc` for verbs touching captured artifacts; for each, classify per-kind variance (unchanged / split / retired). Yoke is the headline because consumer-landing differs per kind; other splits expected. Recipe, not enumeration — premature listing ages poorly. (Quoin anchors live in `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`: `rbtgo_ark_enshrine`, `rbtgo_depot_inscribe`, `rbtgo_director_yoke`, `gar_enshrines_namespace`/`gar_reliquaries_namespace`, `rbst_reliquary_stamp`, `rbf_fact_reliquary` are the capture cluster.)
- **Substrate election landing.** For `wsl` and `podvm-*`, the touchmark is Director-pinned at the nameplate (determinism premise), materialized at charge — but *which* host-tier regime backs the node-side provisioning (station regime vs BURN node profile vs a new host-config regime) is open. Decide when the first non-vessel Lode consumption pace mounts.
- **Spec letter `RBSL`; code-module prefix `rbld_` (ratified).** `L` verified free against the RBS* tree. The Lode *code* module is **`rbld_`** (constants `RBLD_*`, private `zrbld_*`), completing the system-wide Lode naming set: colophon `l` (`rbw-l*`), spec `RBSL`, GAR namespace `rbi_ld`, code `rbld`. This consciously relaxes the earlier "other than `rbl`" caution: `rbl` is a *pure container* — its only child is `rblm` Lifecycle Marshal, and it never itself names a thing — so terminal exclusivity holds and no coherent theme is diluted; `rbld_` and `rblm_` never collide at the 4-char grep grain. (`rbld_` code vs `rbi_ld` namespace are intentionally close — the module produces the namespace.)
- **GAR layout & provenance attachment — DECIDED** (see *Lode registry layout & naming*): single `rbi_ld`, one-package-per-Lode, members + reserved `:rbi_vouch` tag; `rbi_es`/`rbi_rq` migrate into `rbi_ld` at the deferred cutover. The OCI referrers API remains the future upgrade for signed attestation once GAR maturity is confirmed.

## Test coverage gate before deploy

Lifecycle fixtures against the new Lode surface must be slated and landed before deploy: capture / list / inspect / wrest / delete end-to-end against live GAR per kind, bookended by prereq-sweep at head and muster-absent at tail. The bar forks by kind: `base`/`tool`/`reliquary` carry their consumption-adjacent paths; `wsl`/`podvm` stop at the registry — **capture → list → inspect → per-member + whole-Lode delete against live GAR, no host in the loop** (consumption deferred). Whole-Lode delete is a single `packages delete` — the regression risk shifts to the **per-member delete path** (the cleanup-after-bugs case): multi-member kinds must carry concrete member-absence assertions after a partial delete, since that is where a package can be left in a partial state. The fixture surface scales with the operation surface; test paces are not optional polish. Manual operator testing during planning is the bridge, not a substitute.

## Open issues before mounting paces

Remaining open items are pace-time choices, settled by the first relevant pace:

1. **Host-tier regime** for substrate touchmark election/provisioning (`wsl`/`podvm`) — station regime vs BURN node profile vs a new host-config regime. Decide when the first substrate-kind consumption pace mounts.

(GAR layout and provenance attachment, formerly open here, are now decided — see *Lode registry layout & naming*: single `rbi_ld`, one-package-per-Lode, reserved `:rbi_vouch` tag.)

Planning/design heat — no paces yet. Paddock encodes the model and the recipes for follow-up work; the remaining landing-regime decisions defer until paces commit, when everything here is re-investigated. Scope locked to the fetched side (made-images out of scope; see Shape) and to acquisition (wsl/podvm consumption deferred — this heat captures their bytes into GAR and proves control there; host consumption is the locked-but-deferred sequel). Heat silks are `rbk-16-mvp-lode-universal-capture`.

# jjezs_pace ₢BHAAA

## Character
First kind sets the reusable shape; build with live-GAR verification. Sequential.

## Goal
Implement base-kind capture (`ensconce`) as an additive module, parallel-safe alongside live `enshrine`, per the locked Lode shape in the paddock. No cutover.

## Starting state
Gate passed — `enshrine` is cleanly separable: write path is namespace-parameterized (`RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT`, no hardcoded `rbi_es`), ANCHOR consumption is read-only, audit enumerators are independent per-namespace functions (a new namespace stays invisible until its own enumerator exists — see the `rbi_df` precedent), and conjure's in-pool preflight is not namespace-aware. Design locked in paddock *Lode registry layout & naming* — build to it, don't re-litigate.

## Build
- **Three first-class `l`-family commands this pace**, not capture alone: `ensconce` (`lE`, capture), `divine` (`ld`, lowercase read-only enumerate-by-touchmark), `banish` (`lB`, whole-Lode `packages delete`). The `service` fixture drives the real commands, not test-only scaffolding.
- Code-module prefix is **`rbld_`** (ratified — see paddock); spec letter `RBSL` reserved.
- Capture-pure `ensconce`: resolve coordinate from the vessel ORIGIN convention -> fetch + measure digest inside Cloud Build (egress pool, never the workstation) -> push the `rbi_ld` Lode with its member tags + `:rbi_vouch` -> emit the host capture-file. Writes NO consumer config; never touches `enshrine` or ANCHOR (the enshrine rbrv.env writeback is exactly what capture-pure drops).
- Design the 1-member package so an N-member cohort slots in without restructuring (the cheap test of "cardinality is just an attribute").
- `RBSL` spec stub only if useful — do not finish it; RBS0* edits are out of scope (deferred to the remainder pace).

## Build recipe — templates to copy-adapt
`ensconce` mirrors `enshrine` structurally. Templates: the `rbfd_enshrine` orchestrator family in `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` (host orchestrate -> stitch build JSON -> extract output) and its in-pool step `rbgje/rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`; the depot-family enrollment block in `rbz_zipper.sh` (mirror as a new `rbw-l*` Lode group); a `tt/rbw-dE.*.sh` launcher; and the `fast`/`service` fixtures in `rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs` / `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` (+ fixture-name + required-colophons in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, then register in the `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` array). The Lode category constant slots beside the existing four in `rbgc_Constants.sh` + `rbgl_GarLayout.sh`. For sourcing/kindle, copy a `*_cli.sh` furnish wrapper.

## Tests (backbone, not coverage)
- `fast` (deterministic): coordinate resolution, digest verification, envelope-shape over fixtures — assert capture-fidelity (recorded digest == fixture upstream digest), not existence.
- `service` (live GAR, guaranteed teardown): capture -> list -> inspect -> whole-Lode delete; assert the mixed-artifact package (member tags + `:rbi_vouch`) round-trips and is reaped. Single-call `packages delete` preferred; a GCP-run tag-delete loop is an acceptable fallback if GAR refuses.
- minimal upstream smoke — upstream is the Pale; contain its nondeterminism at one membrane.

## Done
`ensconce` captures a base into a parallel `rbi_ld` Lode with member tags + a real `:rbi_vouch` envelope + host capture-file; `divine` lists / inspects and `banish` deletes, all working against live GAR; the fast/service/smoke layers exist for base; live `enshrine` and all conjure builds remain untouched and green.

content.text:
Mounted. Here's where ₣BH stands and what ₢BHAAA asks for.

## Pace goal — ₢BHAAA (lode-base-pilot-sketch) `[rough]`

**Build the first fetched-side capture kind: a new, additive `l`-family module that captures an upstream base image into a parallel `rbi_ld` GAR "Lode" package — with member tags + a signed `:rbi_vouch` provenance envelope + a host capture-file — running entirely cloud-side, without touching the live `enshrine` path or writing any consumer config (capture-pure). Three commands ship together: `ensconce` (capture), `divine` (read-only enumerate), `banish` (whole-Lode delete), plus fast/service/smoke test layers.**

## Heat context

**₣BH — rbk-mvp-lode-universal-capture** (racing, 0/2): broaden the narrow `enshrine` base-OCI-mirror into project-controlled capture across heterogeneous kinds (base, tool, reliquary, wsl, podvm), unified by the **output** — one `Lode` package noun + one capture-file envelope — not by the verb (capture forks per-kind; `enshrine` retires). This pace is the first kind, establishing the reusable shape. Scope is the fetched side only, acquisition only (wsl/podvm consumption deferred).

Recent work has all been paddock-curry — the design is locked (Lode registry layout, `rbld_` module prefix, the verb set, the `divine`/`audit` grain split). This is the first pace that turns design into code.

## Name assessment

The silks **lode-base-pilot-sketch** undersells the docket. "Pilot" is apt (first kind, sets the shape), but "sketch" implies something exploratory — whereas the **Done** bar is three working commands against **live GAR** with fast/service/smoke layers. That's a build, not a sketch. If you agree, I'd offer `lode-base-ensconce-trio` or `lode-base-first-kind`. Not load-bearing — flag only.

## Proposed approach

- **Foundation**: add the `rbi_ld` category constant beside the existing four (`rbgc_Constants.sh` + `rbgl_GarLayout.sh`); stand up the `rbld_` module via a `*_cli.sh` furnish wrapper, structurally mirroring the `rbfd_enshrine` family.
- **Three commands, capture-pure**: `ensconce` resolves the coordinate from the vessel ORIGIN convention → fetches + measures digest *in Cloud Build* (egress pool) → pushes the one-package `rbi_ld` Lode with member tags + `:rbi_vouch` → emits the host capture-file, writing **no** consumer config; `divine` enumerates Lodes by touchmark (lowercase, read-only); `banish` does the whole-Lode `packages delete`. Design the 1-member package so an N-member cohort slots in unchanged.
- **Wire-up**: mirror the depot-family enrollment block in `rbz_zipper.sh` as a new `rbw-l*` group; add `tt/rbw-l*` launchers; register fast + service fixtures (`rbtdrf_fast.rs` / `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` + manifest + `RBTDRC_FIXTURES`).
- **Verify**: fast deterministic layer (capture-fidelity: recorded digest == fixture upstream digest), then a live-GAR `service` round-trip (capture → list → inspect → whole-Lode delete, mixed-artifact package round-trips and is reaped), with `enshrine` + all conjure builds left untouched and green.

## Execution strategy

**Sequential, opus, single thread.** The Character line mandates sequential, and the work is a genuine dependency chain — the `rbld_` module is the spine; constants feed it, commands build on it, zipper/tabtargets expose it, fixtures exercise it. Little file independence to parallelize, and it's architectural (new module, GAR layout, provenance envelope shape) — opus territory, not mechanical. The `service` fixture needs GCP credentials for the live-GAR round-trip; the `fast` layer runs without.

One open question worth settling before I touch code: **how much of this you want to drive interactively vs. hand me to run.** Given "rough" status and that this is the shape-setting pace, you may want to walk the `ensconce` cloud-side pipeline and the `:rbi_vouch` field serialization together rather than have me commit a first cut. Want me to proceed with the build, or start by sketching the `ensconce` orchestrator + envelope shape for your review first?

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GAR Layout - Implementation
#
# Kindle constants for GAR categorical-namespace path construction.
# Callers interpolate the three root constants with hallmark/date/anchor and
# basename literals at the call site — no subshell, no function call overhead.
#
# Shape:
#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/<hallmark>/<basename>:<hallmark>"
#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/<date>/<tool>:<date>"
#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/<anchor>:<anchor>"
#   "${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT}/<filename>:<tag>"

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBGL_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgl multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGL_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgl_*)

zrbgl_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGL_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgl already kindled"

  # Category constants come from rbgc.
  zrbgc_sentinel

  # Root segments — category only. Callers append '/<id>/<basename>:<tag>'.
  readonly RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}"
  readonly RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES}"
  readonly RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES}"
  readonly RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS}"

  readonly ZRBGL_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgl_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGL_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgl not kindled - call zrbgl_kindle first"
}

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
25	test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
26	ZRBGC_SOURCED=1
27	
28	# Tinder constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
29	# Depot project ID infix between RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX and RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER, consumed
30	# by rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh's RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID derivation.
31	RBGC_depot_project_infix="d-"
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)
35	
36	zrbgc_kindle() {
37	  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"
38	
39	  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
40	  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
41	  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
42	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
43	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
44	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
45	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
46	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
47	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
48	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"
49	
50	  # Global resource validation patterns
51	  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS  (timestamp survives — payor is installation-scoped, not depot-scoped)
52	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
53	
54	  # Basic Configuration
55	  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
56	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
57	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"
58	
59	  # Timeouts
60	  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
61	  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
62	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
63	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10
64	
65	  # JWT-bearer consumer-side retry — every RBRA consumer absorbs the post-write
66	  # race where Google's OAuth backend has not yet accepted a freshly-minted SA
67	  # or its key, surfaced as `invalid_grant` paired with either
68	  # `Invalid JWT Signature.` (fresh-key lag) or `Invalid grant: account not
69	  # found` (fresh-SA lag). 90s budget locked by ₣BB pristine-tier contract;
70	  # cadence below.
71	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC=90
72	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2
73	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC=15
74	
75	  # IAM-grant propagation retry — exponential-backoff budget shared by every
76	  # get-modify-set IAM grant site in rbgi_IAM.sh plus the inline GAR retry in
77	  # rbgg_invest_director. Recognizes three propagation classes against the
78	  # same time budget: (A) forward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "does not
79	  # exist"), (B) backward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "is not deleted"),
80	  # (C) caller-recently-empowered (HTTP 403 from resource-scope IAM caches).
81	  # Class C is time-bounded only — real propagation succeeds within budget,
82	  # real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. RBSCIP locks the profile.
83	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=3
84	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC=20
85	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC=420
86	
87	  # HTTP transient-failure retry — bounded retry on curl-network blips
88	  # (connection refused 7, timeout 28, TLS handshake 35, recv failure 56).
89	  # Shared by rbuh_json and the OAuth token-mint POST. Other curl exits
90	  # are configuration-deterministic and fail fast.
91	  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3
92	  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC=3
93	
94	  # docker login daemon->registry transient — the moby/moby#44350 signature.
95	  # docker login's registry-auth client carries a hardcoded, non-configurable
96	  # 15s timeout (moby/registry/auth.go); against a healthy-but-slow GAR auth
97	  # backend it fires prematurely, emitting this Go net/http stdlib string.
98	  # Alone among login/pull/push, login carries no internal retry, so callers
99	  # wrap it (rbgo_docker_login, zrbndb_docker_login) reusing the HTTP retry
100	  # budget above. The string is a Go standard-library invariant, stable across
101	  # docker versions; this is a surveyed-signature allowlist, NOT a catch-all —
102	  # real auth failures emit "unauthorized" and fail fast.
103	  readonly RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE='Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers'
104	
105	  # URL Roots & Well-known Endpoints
106	  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL="https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
107	  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_AUTHORIZE_URL="https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
108	  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_USERINFO_URL="https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/userinfo"
109	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM="https://iam.googleapis.com"
110	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM="https://cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
111	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com"
112	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
113	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com"
114	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING="https://cloudbilling.googleapis.com"
115	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE="https://storage.googleapis.com"
116	  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER="https://secretmanager.googleapis.com"
117	  readonly RBGC_CONSOLE_URL="https://console.cloud.google.com/"
118	  readonly RBGC_SIGNUP_URL="https://cloud.google.com/free"
119	
120	  # OAuth Scopes
121	  readonly RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM="https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"
122	
123	  # Service Usage Service Identifiers
124	  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_IAM="iam.googleapis.com"
125	  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_CRM="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
126	  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
127	
128	  # Email/Domain Assembly
129	  readonly RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN="iam.gserviceaccount.com"
130	
131	  # API Version Paths
132	  readonly RBGC_IAM_V1="/v1"
133	  readonly RBGC_CRM_V1="/v1"
134	  readonly RBGC_CRM_V3="/v3"
135	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1="/v1"
136	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1="/v1beta1"
137	  readonly RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1="/v1"
138	  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1="/v1"
139	  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1="/v1"
140	  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1="/storage/v1"
141	  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_UPLOAD="/upload/storage/v1"
142	  readonly RBGC_SECRETMANAGER_V1="/v1"
143	
144	  # REST Path Fragments
145	  readonly RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS="/projects"
146	  readonly RBGC_PATH_LOCATIONS="/locations"
147	  readonly RBGC_PATH_REPOSITORIES="/repositories"
148	  readonly RBGC_PATH_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS="/serviceAccounts"
149	  readonly RBGC_PATH_KEYS="/keys"
150	
151	  # REST Operation Suffixes
152	  readonly RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":getIamPolicy"
153	  readonly RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":setIamPolicy"
154	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX=":enable"
155	  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_PATH_SERVICES="/services"
156	
157	  # Operation Prefixes
158	  readonly RBGC_OP_PREFIX_GLOBAL="operations/"
159	
160	  # Ark Artifact Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
161	  # Each ark type is a plain basename sibling under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/.
162	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE="image"
163	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT="about"
164	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH="vouch"
165	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS="diags"
166	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST="attest"
167	  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH="pouch"
168	
169	  # Hallmark Prefix Letters
170	  # Encode artifact provenance in the leading character of a hallmark stamp.
171	  # Kludge hallmarks are local-only; the other three originate in GAR.
172	  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_CONJURE="c"
173	  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE="k"
174	  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_BIND="b"
175	  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_GRAFT="g"
176	
177	  # GAR Categorical Namespaces (₢A_AAK layout)
178	  # Top-level namespaces under which arks are stored. Consumed by rbgl_GarLayout.sh.
179	  # The hm/rq/es siblings hold Director-authored image families; rbi_df holds
180	  # Payor-authored depot-scoped OCI artifacts produced during depot lifetime.
181	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="rbi_hm"
182	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="rbi_rq"
183	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES="rbi_es"
184	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"
185	
186	  # rbi_df layout: flat namespace. No subdirs. Each filename names one
187	  # depot-scoped artifact; tag varies by artifact role.
188	  #
189	  # Current artifacts:
190	  #   probe-tether:probe   — tether pool levy-time capability probe
191	  #                          (cloud-pushed marker image, FROM scratch)
192	  #   probe-airgap:probe   — airgap pool levy-time capability probe
193	  #                          (cloud-pushed marker image, FROM scratch)
194	  #   rbrd:tripwire        — depot regime tripwire (FROM-scratch image
195	  #                          carrying rbmm_moorings/rbrd.env). Host-inscribed by
196	  #                          Payor at end of levy; pulled + byte-diffed
197	  #                          by every cloud-submitting command. See
198	  #                          Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh.
199	  #
200	  # The depot-time-immutable identity and pool settings (CLOUD_PREFIX,
201	  # DEPOT_MONIKER, GCP_REGION, GCB_MACHINE_TYPE) live in the RBRD regime
202	  # and are also inscribed into rbi_df at the rbrd:tripwire tag so post-
203	  # levy drift can be detected at every subsequent cloud submission.
204	  #
205	  # Enumerators (rbw-iah / rbw-iar / rbw-iae) ignore rbi_df by design — its
206	  # contents are operational, not part of the hallmark/reliquary/enshrine
207	  # image catalogue.
208	
209	  # Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
210	  # Canonical tool names under rbi_rq/<date>/. Authoritative
211	  # manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
212	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
213	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
214	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
215	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
216	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
217	  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"
218	
219	  # Fact-file filenames (written to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by producers, read by tests)
220	  readonly RBF_FACT_HALLMARK="rbf_fact_hallmark"
221	  readonly RBF_FACT_BUILD_ID="rbf_fact_build_id"
222	  readonly RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT="rbf_fact_gar_root"
223	  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM="rbf_fact_ark_stem"
224	  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_YIELD="rbf_fact_ark_yield"
225	  readonly RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY="rbf_fact_reliquary"
226	
227	  # Payor fact-file filenames (governor identifying values)
228	  readonly RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL="rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email"
229	
230	  # Depot lifecycle-state vocabulary. Fact-file extensions live in RBCC.
231	  # rbgp_depot_list emits one fact file per known depot at
232	  # "<cloud_prefix>/<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}" with content equal to
233	  # one of the values below. The cloud_prefix subdir prevents collisions
234	  # between same-moniker depots under different cloud_prefixes.
235	  readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE="COMPLETE"
236	  readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED="DELETE_REQUESTED"
237	
238	  # DisplayName anchor used across depot-creation sites (depot project,
239	  # Mason SA, Governor SA). Search backend filters CRM v3 projects:search
240	  # by displayName starting with this anchor. Distinct, unmistakable string
241	  # ensures no collision with non-depot projects in the operator's account.
242	  readonly RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX="RBGC-DEPOT"
243	
244	  # Artifact Registry (GAR) Composition
245	  readonly RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX="-docker.pkg.dev"
246	
247	  # GAR Cleanup Policy (applied at depot levy — see RBSDE "Create Container Repository").
248	  # Reaps untagged manifests on GAR's daily cleanup cadence; underwrites the V2-DELETE-by-tag
249	  # contract documented in RBSIJ for multi-platform orphan children.
250	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CLEANUP_POLICY_ID="rb-delete-untagged"
251	  readonly RBGC_GAR_CLEANUP_OLDER_THAN_SEC="86400s"
252	
253	
254	  # Canonical Role IDs
255	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER="roles/artifactregistry.reader"
256	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_WRITER="roles/artifactregistry.writer"
257	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_ADMIN="roles/artifactregistry.admin"
258	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER="roles/containeranalysis.occurrences.viewer"
259	  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR="roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor"
260	
261	  # Common API Base Paths (project-independent)
262	  readonly RBGC_API_BASE_GCS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}"
263	
264	  # Cloud Resource Manager - Liens API
265	  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_LIST_LIENS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
266	  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
267	
268	  # Google Cloud Storage (GCS) APIs (project-independent)
269	  readonly RBGC_API_GCS_BUCKETS="${RBGC_API_BASE_GCS}/b"
270	
271	  readonly RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM="linux/amd64"
272	
273	
274	  # Worker pool infrastructure (dual pools: tether + airgap)
275	  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER="-tether"
276	  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP="-airgap"
277	  readonly RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX="-pool"
278	  readonly RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS="/workerPools"
279	
280	  readonly ZRBGC_KINDLED=1
281	}
282	
283	zrbgc_sentinel() {
284	  test "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgc not kindled - call zrbgc_kindle first"
285	}
286	
287	# eof
288	
289

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGC_SOURCED=1

# Tinder constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
# Depot project ID infix between RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX and RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER, consumed
# by rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh's RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID derivation.
RBGC_depot_project_infix="d-"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)

zrbgc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"

  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"

  # Global resource validation patterns
  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS  (timestamp survives — payor is installation-scoped, not depot-scoped)
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"

  # Basic Configuration
  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"

  # Timeouts
  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10

  # JWT-bearer consumer-side retry — every RBRA consumer absorbs the post-write
  # race where Google's OAuth backend has not yet accepted a freshly-minted SA
  # or its key, surfaced as `invalid_grant` paired with either
  # `Invalid JWT Signature.` (fresh-key lag) or `Invalid grant: account not
  # found` (fresh-SA lag). 90s budget locked by ₣BB pristine-tier contract;
  # cadence below.
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC=90
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC=15

  # IAM-grant propagation retry — exponential-backoff budget shared by every
  # get-modify-set IAM grant site in rbgi_IAM.sh plus the inline GAR retry in
  # rbgg_invest_director. Recognizes three propagation classes against the
  # same time budget: (A) forward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "does not
  # exist"), (B) backward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "is not deleted"),
  # (C) caller-recently-empowered (HTTP 403 from resource-scope IAM caches).
  # Class C is time-bounded only — real propagation succeeds within budget,
  # real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. RBSCIP locks the profile.
  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=3
  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC=20
  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC=420

  # HTTP transient-failure retry — bounded retry on curl-network blips
  # (connection refused 7, timeout 28, TLS handshake 35, recv failure 56).
  # Shared by rbuh_json and the OAuth token-mint POST. Other curl exits
  # are configuration-deterministic and fail fast.
  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3
  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC=3

  # docker login daemon->registry transient — the moby/moby#44350 signature.
  # docker login's registry-auth client carries a hardcoded, non-configurable
  # 15s timeout (moby/registry/auth.go); against a healthy-but-slow GAR auth
  # backend it fires prematurely, emitting this Go net/http stdlib string.
  # Alone among login/pull/push, login carries no internal retry, so callers
  # wrap it (rbgo_docker_login, zrbndb_docker_login) reusing the HTTP retry
  # budget above. The string is a Go standard-library invariant, stable across
  # docker versions; this is a surveyed-signature allowlist, NOT a catch-all —
  # real auth failures emit "unauthorized" and fail fast.
  readonly RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE='Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers'

  # URL Roots & Well-known Endpoints
  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL="https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_AUTHORIZE_URL="https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_USERINFO_URL="https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/userinfo"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM="https://iam.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM="https://cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING="https://cloudbilling.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE="https://storage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER="https://secretmanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_CONSOLE_URL="https://console.cloud.google.com/"
  readonly RBGC_SIGNUP_URL="https://cloud.google.com/free"

  # OAuth Scopes
  readonly RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM="https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"

  # Service Usage Service Identifiers
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_IAM="iam.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_CRM="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="artifactregistry.googleapis.com"

  # Email/Domain Assembly
  readonly RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN="iam.gserviceaccount.com"

  # API Version Paths
  readonly RBGC_IAM_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_V3="/v3"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1="/v1beta1"
  readonly RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1="/storage/v1"
  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_UPLOAD="/upload/storage/v1"
  readonly RBGC_SECRETMANAGER_V1="/v1"

  # REST Path Fragments
  readonly RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS="/projects"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_LOCATIONS="/locations"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_REPOSITORIES="/repositories"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS="/serviceAccounts"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_KEYS="/keys"

  # REST Operation Suffixes
  readonly RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":getIamPolicy"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":setIamPolicy"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX=":enable"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_PATH_SERVICES="/services"

  # Operation Prefixes
  readonly RBGC_OP_PREFIX_GLOBAL="operations/"

  # Ark Artifact Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Each ark type is a plain basename sibling under rbi_hm/<hallmark>/.
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE="image"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT="about"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH="vouch"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS="diags"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST="attest"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH="pouch"

  # Hallmark Prefix Letters
  # Encode artifact provenance in the leading character of a hallmark stamp.
  # Kludge hallmarks are local-only; the other three originate in GAR.
  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_CONJURE="c"
  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_KLUDGE="k"
  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_BIND="b"
  readonly RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_GRAFT="g"

  # GAR Categorical Namespaces (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Top-level namespaces under which arks are stored. Consumed by rbgl_GarLayout.sh.
  # The hm/rq/es siblings hold Director-authored image families; rbi_df holds
  # Payor-authored depot-scoped OCI artifacts produced during depot lifetime.
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="rbi_hm"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="rbi_rq"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES="rbi_es"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"

  # rbi_df layout: flat namespace. No subdirs. Each filename names one
  # depot-scoped artifact; tag varies by artifact role.
  #
  # Current artifacts:
  #   probe-tether:probe   — tether pool levy-time capability probe
  #                          (cloud-pushed marker image, FROM scratch)
  #   probe-airgap:probe   — airgap pool levy-time capability probe
  #                          (cloud-pushed marker image, FROM scratch)
  #   rbrd:tripwire        — depot regime tripwire (FROM-scratch image
  #                          carrying rbmm_moorings/rbrd.env). Host-inscribed by
  #                          Payor at end of levy; pulled + byte-diffed
  #                          by every cloud-submitting command. See
  #                          Tools/rbk/rbndb_base.sh.
  #
  # The depot-time-immutable identity and pool settings (CLOUD_PREFIX,
  # DEPOT_MONIKER, GCP_REGION, GCB_MACHINE_TYPE) live in the RBRD regime
  # and are also inscribed into rbi_df at the rbrd:tripwire tag so post-
  # levy drift can be detected at every subsequent cloud submission.
  #
  # Enumerators (rbw-iah / rbw-iar / rbw-iae) ignore rbi_df by design — its
  # contents are operational, not part of the hallmark/reliquary/enshrine
  # image catalogue.

  # Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Canonical tool names under rbi_rq/<date>/. Authoritative
  # manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"

  # Fact-file filenames (written to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by producers, read by tests)
  readonly RBF_FACT_HALLMARK="rbf_fact_hallmark"
  readonly RBF_FACT_BUILD_ID="rbf_fact_build_id"
  readonly RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT="rbf_fact_gar_root"
  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM="rbf_fact_ark_stem"
  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_YIELD="rbf_fact_ark_yield"
  readonly RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY="rbf_fact_reliquary"

  # Payor fact-file filenames (governor identifying values)
  readonly RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL="rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email"

  # Depot lifecycle-state vocabulary. Fact-file extensions live in RBCC.
  # rbgp_depot_list emits one fact file per known depot at
  # "<cloud_prefix>/<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}" with content equal to
  # one of the values below. The cloud_prefix subdir prevents collisions
  # between same-moniker depots under different cloud_prefixes.
  readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE="COMPLETE"
  readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED="DELETE_REQUESTED"

  # DisplayName anchor used across depot-creation sites (depot project,
  # Mason SA, Governor SA). Search backend filters CRM v3 projects:search
  # by displayName starting with this anchor. Distinct, unmistakable string
  # ensures no collision with non-depot projects in the operator's account.
  readonly RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX="RBGC-DEPOT"

  # Artifact Registry (GAR) Composition
  readonly RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX="-docker.pkg.dev"

  # GAR Cleanup Policy (applied at depot levy — see RBSDE "Create Container Repository").
  # Reaps untagged manifests on GAR's daily cleanup cadence; underwrites the V2-DELETE-by-tag
  # contract documented in RBSIJ for multi-platform orphan children.
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CLEANUP_POLICY_ID="rb-delete-untagged"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CLEANUP_OLDER_THAN_SEC="86400s"


  # Canonical Role IDs
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER="roles/artifactregistry.reader"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_WRITER="roles/artifactregistry.writer"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_ADMIN="roles/artifactregistry.admin"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER="roles/containeranalysis.occurrences.viewer"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR="roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor"

  # Common API Base Paths (project-independent)
  readonly RBGC_API_BASE_GCS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}"

  # Cloud Resource Manager - Liens API
  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_LIST_LIENS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"

  # Google Cloud Storage (GCS) APIs (project-independent)
  readonly RBGC_API_GCS_BUCKETS="${RBGC_API_BASE_GCS}/b"

  readonly RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM="linux/amd64"


  # Worker pool infrastructure (dual pools: tether + airgap)
  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER="-tether"
  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP="-airgap"
  readonly RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX="-pool"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS="/workerPools"

  readonly ZRBGC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgc not kindled - call zrbgc_kindle first"
}

# eof

36	zrbfd_kindle() {
37	  test -z "${ZRBFD_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfd already kindled"
38	
39	  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'
40	  zrbfc_kindle
41	
42	  buc_log_args 'Verify service account files'
43	  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
44	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
45	
46	  buc_log_args 'RBGJ files in same Tools directory as this implementation'
47	  # Acronym: rbgjb = Recipe Bottle Google Json Build (step scripts in rbgjb/ dir)
48	  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
49	  readonly ZRBFD_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjb"
50	  test -d "${ZRBFD_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJB steps directory not found: ${ZRBFD_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR}"
51	
52	  # RBGJV and RBGJA step dirs now owned by rbfc_FoundryCore.sh (shared assembly helpers)
53	
54	  buc_log_args 'RBGJM mirror step scripts (same Tools directory)'
55	  # Acronym: rbgjm = Recipe Bottle Google Json Mirror (step scripts in rbgjm/ dir)
56	  readonly ZRBFD_RBGJM_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjm"
57	  test -d "${ZRBFD_RBGJM_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJM steps directory not found: ${ZRBFD_RBGJM_STEPS_DIR}"
58	
59	  buc_log_args 'RBGJE enshrine step scripts (same Tools directory)'
60	  # Acronym: rbgje = Recipe Bottle Google Json Enshrine (step scripts in rbgje/ dir)
61	  readonly ZRBFD_RBGJE_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgje"
62	  test -d "${ZRBFD_RBGJE_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJE steps directory not found: ${ZRBFD_RBGJE_STEPS_DIR}"
63	
64	  # RBGJI inscribe step scripts now owned by rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh
65	
66	  # Delete, token, inscribe, reliquary prefixes now owned by rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh
67	  # Vouch and about prefixes now owned by rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh
68	
69	  buc_log_args 'Define stitch operation file prefix (postfixed per step id)'
70	  readonly ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_stitch_"
71	
72	  # Inscribe and reliquary prefixes now owned by rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh
73	
74	  buc_log_args 'Define mirror operation files'
75	  readonly ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_mirror_"
76	
77	  buc_log_args 'Define graft operation files'
78	  readonly ZRBFD_GRAFT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_graft_"
79	
80	  buc_log_args 'Define enshrine operation files'
81	  readonly ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_enshrine_"
82	
83	  buc_log_args 'Define enshrine preflight files'
84	  readonly ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_preflight_"
85	
86	  buc_log_args 'Define context push operation files'
87	  readonly ZRBFD_CONTEXT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_context_"
88	
89	  buc_log_args 'Kindle verify module (cross-module calls from ordain)'
90	  zrbfv_kindle
91	
92	  readonly ZRBFD_KINDLED=1
93	}

95	zrbfd_sentinel() {
96	  zrbfc_sentinel
97	  test "${ZRBFD_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfd not kindled - call zrbfd_kindle first"
98	}

101	# Verify reliquary tool images exist in GAR.
102	# Full-subtree presence check: every canonical tool image must be present at
103	# the stamp. Inscribe creates all 6 atomically; piecemeal jettison or registry
104	# damage compromises the stamp's integrity. Misses are accumulated across the
105	# whole tool set so the operator sees full damage in one report.
106	# Args: token vessel_dir
107	zrbfd_preflight_reliquary() {
108	  zrbfd_sentinel
109	
110	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
111	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
112	  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: token required"
113	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: vessel_dir required"
114	
115	  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
116	  test -n "${z_reliquary}" || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY required on every ordain-path vessel — yoke a reliquary stamp via tt/${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}.sh before ordaining"
117	
118	  buc_step "Verifying reliquary tool images exist in GAR"
119	
120	  local -r z_canonical_tools=(
121	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}"
122	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}"
123	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}"
124	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}"
125	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}"
126	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}"
127	  )
128	
129	  local z_missing=()
130	  local z_tool=""
131	  local z_pkg=""
132	  local z_status_file=""
133	  local z_response_file=""
134	  local z_stderr_file=""
135	  local z_http_code=""
136	
137	  for z_tool in "${z_canonical_tools[@]}"; do
138	    z_pkg="${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
139	    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_status.txt"
140	    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_response.txt"
141	    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_stderr.txt"
142	
143	    curl --head -sS \
144	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
145	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
146	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
147	      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
148	      -w "%{http_code}" \
149	      -o "${z_response_file}" \
150	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg}/manifests/${z_reliquary}" \
151	      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
152	      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
153	
154	    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
155	    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for reliquary check: ${z_tool}"
156	
157	    case "${z_http_code}" in
158	      200) buc_log_args "Reliquary tool present: ${z_tool}" ;;
159	      404) z_missing+=("${z_tool}") ;;
160	      *)   buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary}" ;;
161	    esac
162	  done
163	
164	  if test "${#z_missing[@]}" -eq 0; then
165	    buc_info "Reliquary verified: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_canonical_tools[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools present)"
166	    return 0
167	  fi
168	
169	  buc_warn "Reliquary integrity check failed: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_missing[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools missing)"
170	  buc_bare "  The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images (gcloud, docker,"
171	  buc_bare "  syft, alpine, binfmt, skopeo) inscribed from upstream into your private GAR."
172	  buc_bare "  Air-gapped worker pools cannot pull from the public internet — the reliquary"
173	  buc_bare "  stages these tools so builds can run without egress. Piecemeal jettison is"
174	  buc_bare "  allowed but unrecoverable surgically: re-inscribe the whole stamp."
175	  buc_bare ""
176	  for z_tool in "${z_missing[@]}"; do
177	    buc_bare "  PRECHECK: GAR image not found at ${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
178	    buc_bare "    Required by ${RBRV_SIGIL}'s RBRV_RELIQUARY=${z_reliquary}."
179	  done
180	  buc_bare ""
181	  buc_bare "  Recover by re-inscribing the reliquary, then re-yoking and re-ordaining:"
182	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY}"
183	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}" "<new-stamp>"
184	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
185	  buc_die "Registry preflight failed — ${#z_missing[@]} of ${#z_canonical_tools[@]} reliquary tool images missing from GAR"
186	}

189	# Check concurrent build quota against regime requirements
190	# Args: token mode
191	#   mode: "gate" (die if insufficient) or "advisory" (warn if insufficient)
192	zrbfd_quota_preflight() {
193	  zrbfd_sentinel
194	
195	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
196	
197	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbfd_quota_preflight: token required"
198	
199	  # Extract vCPU count from machine type (last segment after final hyphen)
200	  local -r z_vcpus="${RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE##*-}"
201	  case "${z_vcpus}" in
202	    ""|0|*[!0-9]*)
203	      buc_warn "Cannot parse vCPU count from RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE='${RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}' -- skipping quota preflight"
204	      return 0
205	      ;;
206	  esac
207	
208	  buc_log_args "Machine type ${RBRD_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE} = ${z_vcpus} vCPUs"
209	
210	  # Query Service Usage consumer quota API for concurrent_private_pool_build_cpus
211	  local -r z_metric_encoded="cloudbuild.googleapis.com%2Fconcurrent_private_pool_build_cpus"
212	  local -r z_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/services/cloudbuild.googleapis.com/consumerQuotaMetrics/${z_metric_encoded}"
213	
214	  buc_step "Checking concurrent build quota"
215	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "quota_preflight"
216	
217	  local z_code=""
218	  z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "quota_preflight") || z_code=""
219	  if test "${z_code}" != "200"; then
220	    buc_warn "Could not query build quota (HTTP ${z_code}) -- skipping preflight check"
221	    return 0
222	  fi
223	
224	  # Filter quota response to region-specific bucket via intermediate file
225	  rbuh_jq_file_to_file_ok "quota_preflight" "quota_region" \
226	    "[.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[] | select(.dimensions.region == \"${RBRD_GCP_REGION}\")] | .[0] // {}" \
227	    || true
228	
229	  # Extract effective limit from region bucket, then fallback to first bucket
230	  local z_limit=""
231	  z_limit=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "quota_region" '.effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
232	  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
233	    z_limit=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "quota_preflight" \
234	      '.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[0].effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
235	  fi
236	
237	  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
238	    buc_warn "Could not extract quota limit -- skipping preflight check"
239	    return 0
240	  fi
241	
242	  # -1 means unlimited
243	  if test "${z_limit}" = "-1"; then
244	    buc_info "Quota: unlimited concurrent private pool build CPUs"
245	    return 0
246	  fi
247	
248	  # Compute max concurrent builds
249	  local -r z_max_concurrent=$((z_limit / z_vcpus))
250	
251	  buc_log_args "Quota ${z_limit} vCPUs, machine ${z_vcpus} vCPUs, max concurrent ${z_max_concurrent}, required ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"
252	
253	  if test "${z_max_concurrent}" -lt "${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"; then
254	    buc_warn "Build quota insufficient: ${z_limit} vCPU quota / ${z_vcpus} vCPUs per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
255	    buc_warn "Fresh depots start with a low quota. After some build activity, the Edit Quotas option becomes available."
256	    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_QUOTA_BUILD}"
257	  else
258	    buc_info "Quota OK: ${z_limit} vCPU / ${z_vcpus} per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
259	  fi
260	}

262	# Internal: Verify that all required images exist in GAR before submitting
263	# to Cloud Build. Checks two layers in dependency order:
264	#
265	#   1. Reliquary — co-versioned builder tool images (gcloud, docker, syft, etc.)
266	#      created by inscribe. One reliquary per depot setup. Enshrine depends on it.
267	#   2. Enshrine — upstream base images copied into private GAR, pinned by content
268	#      hash. One enshrine per base image anchor. Multiple vessels sharing the same
269	#      anchor only need one enshrine.
270	#
271	# A missing image at either layer causes a Cloud Build failure minutes later.
272	# This preflight catches it immediately with copy-paste remediation commands.
273	#
274	# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR)
275	# and authentication (needs token for registry API).
276	zrbfd_registry_preflight() {
277	  zrbfd_sentinel
278	
279	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
280	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
281	  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: token required"
282	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: vessel_dir required"
283	
284	  # --- Layer 1: Reliquary tool images ---
285	  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
286	
287	  # --- Layer 2: Enshrined base images ---
288	  # Each vessel declares base images via RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN (upstream tag) and
289	  # RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR (locator: package-path:tag). Enshrine copies the upstream
290	  # image into the enshrine namespace, pinned by content hash. The conjure
291	  # Dockerfile's FROM references the locator-resolved GAR ref, not upstream tag.
292	
293	  buc_step "Verifying enshrined base images exist in GAR"
294	
295	  local z_n=""
296	  local z_anchor_var=""
297	  local z_anchor=""
298	  local z_pkg_path=""
299	  local z_tag=""
300	  local z_origin_var=""
301	  local z_origin=""
302	  local z_any_checked="false"
303	  local z_status_file=""
304	  local z_response_file=""
305	  local z_stderr_file=""
306	  local z_http_code=""
307	
308	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
309	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
310	    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
311	    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
312	    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
313	
314	    # Skip slots without an origin (no base image to enshrine).
315	    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue
316	
317	    # Anchor handling depends on egress mode. Tether passes through (buildx
318	    # fetches upstream at build time). Airgap requires a populated anchor
319	    # because the worker pool has no upstream egress — an empty anchor with
320	    # a non-empty origin guarantees runtime build failure.
321	    if test -z "${z_anchor}"; then
322	      if test "${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE:-}" = "rbnve_airgap"; then
323	        # Two anchor-population paths per RBSAE: enshrine (origin is a public
324	        # upstream) and hallmark-pin (origin names a producer vessel in this
325	        # repo). Discriminate by whether origin resolves to a vessel directory.
326	        if test -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}"; then
327	          buc_warn "Airgap vessel ${RBRV_SIGIL} has empty ${z_anchor_var}; origin ${z_origin} names a producer vessel"
328	          buc_bare "  ${z_anchor_var} is a hallmark-pin, not an enshrine locator — enshrine is not invoked on this vessel."
329	          buc_bare "  Ordain the producer vessel first, then write its hallmark into ${z_anchor_var}."
330	          buc_bare "  Canonical handbook path:"
331	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_AIRGAP}"
332	          buc_bare "  Minimal manual sequence:"
333	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_origin}"
334	          buc_bare "    export PRODUCER_HALLMARK=\$(cat \${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK})"
335	          buc_bare "    # set ${z_anchor_var}=rbi_hm/\${PRODUCER_HALLMARK}/image:\${PRODUCER_HALLMARK}"
336	          buc_bare "    # in ${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env, then:"
337	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
338	          buc_die "Registry preflight failed — airgap vessel missing hallmark-pin anchor"
339	        else
340	          buc_warn "Airgap vessel ${RBRV_SIGIL} has empty ${z_anchor_var} but non-empty ${z_origin_var}=${z_origin}"
341	          buc_bare "  Airgap conjure cannot reach upstream — base images must be enshrined first."
342	          buc_bare "  The anchor locator points at the enshrined image inside GAR. Without it,"
343	          buc_bare "  the airgap worker pool has no source for the base image and the build fails."
344	          buc_bare "  Run enshrine, then re-run ordain:"
345	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
346	          buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
347	          buc_die "Registry preflight failed — airgap vessel missing required anchor"
348	        fi
349	      fi
350	      continue
351	    fi
352	
353	    case "${z_anchor}" in
354	      *:*) : ;;
355	      *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
356	    esac
357	    z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
358	    z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
359	    test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
360	    test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
361	
362	    z_any_checked="true"
363	    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_status.txt"
364	    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_response.txt"
365	    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_stderr.txt"
366	
367	    curl --head -sS \
368	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
369	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
370	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
371	      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
372	      -w "%{http_code}" \
373	      -o "${z_response_file}" \
374	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
375	      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
376	      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for enshrined image: ${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
377	
378	    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
379	    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for enshrine check"
380	
381	    if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
382	      buc_warn "Enshrined base image not found: ${z_anchor} (from ${z_origin})"
383	      buc_bare "  Enshrine copies upstream base images (e.g., busybox:latest from Docker Hub) into"
384	      buc_bare "  your private GAR, pinned by content hash. Like the reliquary, this ensures"
385	      buc_bare "  air-gapped builds never reach the public internet. The anchor locator is stable"
386	      buc_bare "  until you deliberately re-enshrine to pick up a newer upstream version."
387	      buc_bare "  Multiple vessels sharing the same base image anchor need only one enshrine."
388	      buc_bare "  Run enshrine, then re-run ordain:"
389	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
390	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
391	      buc_die "Registry preflight failed — enshrined base image missing from GAR"
392	    elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
393	      buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking enshrined image: ${z_anchor}"
394	    fi
395	
396	    buc_log_args "Enshrined image verified: ${z_anchor}"
397	  done
398	
399	  if test "${z_any_checked}" = "true"; then
400	    buc_info "All enshrined base images verified in GAR"
401	  fi
402	}
